summary: A doctest in `doc/callbacks.rst` at line 48 failed because `estimator.fit(X, y)` returned `LogisticRegression()` instead of the expected progress-bar output string. The progress bar callback was not active during the doctest run, so the fit method returned the estimator repr instead of displaying the progress bar.
root_cause: The doctest in doc/callbacks.rst line 48 expects `estimator.fit(X, y)` to print a Rich progress bar line (`LogisticRegression - fit ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 100% 0:03:12`), but the actual output is `LogisticRegression()` (the default repr), indicating the progress bar callback was not triggered during the doctest execution.

evidence.quote:
048     >>> estimator.fit(X, y)
 Expected:
     LogisticRegression - fit ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 100% 0:03:12
 Got:
     LogisticRegression()
evidence.reason: This is the exact doctest mismatch: the expected output is a progress bar display, but the actual output is the plain estimator repr, meaning the callback/progress bar was not active.
Pytest reports a DocTestFailure at line 48 of callbacks.rst, confirming this is the failing test location.
The short test summary confirms the only failing test is the callbacks.rst doctest.
Only one test failed in the doc-test run, confirming this is the sole root cause of the CI failure.
The `set_callbacks` call is marked `# doctest: +SKIP`, so the progress bar is never actually registered on the estimator before the `fit` call in the doctest, causing the mismatch.

suggested_fix: Either add a `# doctest: +SKIP` directive to the `estimator.fit(X, y)` line in `doc/callbacks.rst` (line 48) to skip the progress-bar output check, or restructure the doctest so the callback is properly registered before `fit` is called (removing the `+SKIP` on `set_callbacks`).
